Assistant Professor active in applied mechanics, robotics, and biorobotics-related teaching and research.

Carol Sergenti is listed by the University of Pavia in the Department of Industrial and Information Engineering in the applied mechanics disciplinary area. The current RAMSLAB roster provided for this site identifies her as Assistant Professor.

Her university profile includes teaching activity in Elements of Biorobotics. That makes her profile especially relevant to laboratory work connecting robotics, mechanics, and human-centered applications.

PhD candidate focused on vision-based motion reconstruction and simulation-oriented human movement analysis.

Davide Todesca appears in the IEIE PhD student list in Industrial Engineering under the supervision of Marco Carnevale and Hermes Giberti.

Official doctoral minutes describe his activity as the study and development of a synchronized monocular camera system for real-time reconstruction of human body segments. For RAMSLAB, this connects his profile to vision systems, motion reconstruction, and simulation-based analysis of movement.

Hermes Giberti is part of the Department of Industrial and Information Engineering at the University of Pavia, where the official UNIFIND profile lists him as Full Professor in the disciplinary area of applied mechanics.

PhD candidate working on dynamic simulation, motion cueing, and hardware-in-the-loop environments for industrial engineering applications.

José Alfonso Farias Fuentes is listed among the IEIE PhD students of the University of Pavia in Industrial Engineering, with Marco Carnevale and Hermes Giberti indicated as supervisors.

The official PhD profile PDF reports prior work on motion cueing algorithm development, while doctoral minutes later reference the development of a hardware-in-the-loop simulation environment and multibody modeling in real time. In RAMSLAB, this places his work around dynamic simulation, control integration, and advanced simulator development.
